timeline.title: Andhra Pradesh SCERT Social Studies History Timeline
timeline.description: Complete historical timeline from Andhra Pradesh SCERT Social Studies syllabus (Grades 6-10)

topics.chapter_name: Ch 6: The Beginnings of Indian Civilisation
topics.subtopic_name: Grid patterns and streets at Harappa and Mohenjo-Daro
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Need for organized urban living due to surplus agriculture. Effect: Efficient movement, sanitation, and social order; influenced later town planning in India.

topics.subtopic_name: The Mauryan Empire – Chandragupta and Ashoka
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Ambition of Chandragupta Maurya and political instability. Effect: First large empire in India, spread of Buddhism under Ashoka.

topics.subtopic_name: Somnath temple raid by Mahmud of Ghazni
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Mahmud of Ghazni's raids for wealth and religious symbolism. Effect: Weakening of temple patronage, loss of wealth, and long-term memory of invasion in Indian historical narratives.

Cause: Invitation by Daulat Khan Lodi and Rana Sanga; weakness of Ibrahim Lodi's army. Effect: Establishment of the Mughal Empire in India, introduction of gunpowder and artillery in North Indian warfare.

Cause: Weak Mughal administration and Nadir Shah's need for wealth. Effect: Plunder of the Peacock Throne and Koh-i-Noor diamond, massive loss of Mughal prestige, and further fragmentation into regional kingdoms.

topics.subtopic_name: Battle of Plassey (1757): Siraj-ud-Daulah vs Clive
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Conflict between Siraj-ud-Daulah and the East India Company over trade rights and fortification. Robert Clive's conspiracy with Mir Jafar. Effect: Company gained vast revenue from Bengal (diwani rights in 1765), military supremacy, and became a political power in India.

topics.subtopic_name: Disobedience and revolt of sepoys in Meerut
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Anger over new rifle cartridges rumored to be greased with animal fat. Effect: Ignited a massive, widespread uprising across North India against British rule.

topics.subtopic_name: End of East India Company rule (1858)
topics.cause_effect: Cause: British failure to suppress 1857 rebellion quickly; realization that Company rule was unstable. Effect: British Crown took direct control (Raj), Queen's Proclamation promised religious tolerance and no further annexations; reorganization of army.

topics.location: Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi, Gujarat, India
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Failure of Simon Commission (1928), rejection of Nehru Report (1928), and Lahore Congress demand for Purna Swaraj (1929). Direct cause: British salt monopoly. Effect: Massive civil disobedience, 60,000+ arrested, Gandhi-Irwin Pact (1931), and participation of women and lower castes in movement.

topics.chapter_name: Ch 8: The Making of the National Movement
topics.cause_effect: Cause: Quit India Movement, WWII impact, British economic exhaustion, and mounting pressure from Indian National Congress. Effect: Independence from British rule, creation of India and Pakistan, followed by partition violence and mass migration.
